---
title: "nitrix.stats.glm"
generated: true
signature-hash: e689ed8e04122f98
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.glm`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map — voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `m` | rows of a multi-row contrast |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `Family` {#Family}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Family -->
```python
class Family
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:Family -->
An exponential-family + link, as a record of pure functions.

Frozen and hashable so it rides as a static config (and a future
`custom_vjp` nondiff arg).  The fields are the GLM/IRLS primitives:

- `link` / `linkinv` -- `g(mu)` and `g^{-1}(eta)`.
- `mu_eta` -- `d mu / d eta` as a function of `eta` (IRLS weight).
- `variance` -- the variance function `V(mu)`.
- `unit_deviance` -- per-observation deviance contribution `d(y, mu)`.
- `init_mu` -- a fitted-mean initialiser from `y` (IRLS start).
- `loglik` -- per-observation log-likelihood `l(y, mu, dispersion)`
  (the dispersion argument is ignored by the fixed-dispersion families).
- `has_fixed_dispersion` -- `True` when the dispersion is 1 (binomial /
  Poisson; negative-binomial with a *known* `alpha`); `False` when it is
  estimated (Gaussian, Gamma).
- `eta_bound` -- the IRLS clamp on the linear predictor (`inf` for the
  bounded identity / logit links; `_ETA_MAX` for the unbounded `exp`
  links, where it stabilises the working response).  The IRLS core
  (:func:`~nitrix.stats._irls._working`) clamps `eta` to `+-eta_bound`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Family.clip_eta -->
```python
clip_eta(eta: Array) -> Array
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

Clamp the linear predictor to the family's numerically-sane range.

`+-eta_bound` -- `inf` for the bounded identity / logit links (a
no-op), `_ETA_MAX` for the unbounded `exp` links (where an
un-clamped `eta` blows up `exp(eta)` and the IRLS weights).  The
single source of truth for the clamp across the IRLS / PQL / Laplace
working-response sites.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Family.with_link -->
```python
with_link(link: Union[str, 'Link']) -> 'Family'
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

A copy of this family with a **non-canonical** link substituted.

Only the link triple (`link` / `linkinv` / `mu_eta`) and the IRLS
`eta_bound` change; the distribution -- `variance` / `unit_deviance`
/ `loglik` / `init_mu` (all functions of `mu`, not `eta`) -- is
untouched, so e.g. `BINOMIAL.with_link('probit')` is the probit model
and `POISSON.with_link('sqrt')` the square-root-link Poisson, exactly as
in `statsmodels` `Family(link=...)` / mgcv.  The shared IRLS core is
Fisher scoring (`w = mu_eta^2 / V`, `z = eta + (y - mu) / mu_eta`), so
any link composes without a solver change.

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Family.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(name: str, has_fixed_dispersion: bool, link: Callable[[Array], Array], linkinv: Callable[[Array], Array], mu_eta: Callable[[Array], Array], variance: Callable[[Array], Array], unit_deviance: Callable[[Array, Array], Array], init_mu: Callable[[Array], Array], loglik: Callable[[Array, Array, Array], Array], eta_bound: float = float('inf')) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `Link` {#Link}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Link -->
```python
class Link
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:Link -->
A link function as a record of its three IRLS primitives.

`link` = `g(mu)`, `linkinv` = `g^{-1}(eta)`, `mu_eta` = `d mu /
d eta` (as a function of `eta`); `eta_bound` is the IRLS linear-predictor
clamp (`inf` unless the inverse link can overflow).  Compose onto a
distribution with :meth:`Family.with_link`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:Link.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(name: str, link: Callable[[Array], Array], linkinv: Callable[[Array], Array], mu_eta: Callable[[Array], Array], eta_bound: float = float('inf')) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `IDENTITY_LINK` {#IDENTITY_LINK}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:IDENTITY_LINK -->
```python
IDENTITY_LINK = Link(name='identity', link=_identity, linkinv=_identity, mu_eta=_ones_like)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:IDENTITY_LINK -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `LOG_LINK` {#LOG_LINK}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:LOG_LINK -->
```python
LOG_LINK = Link(name='log', link=_log_link, linkinv=_safe_exp, mu_eta=_safe_exp, eta_bound=_ETA_MAX)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:LOG_LINK -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `LOGIT_LINK` {#LOGIT_LINK}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:LOGIT_LINK -->
```python
LOGIT_LINK = Link(name='logit', link=_logit, linkinv=_expit, mu_eta=_binomial_mu_eta)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:LOGIT_LINK -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `PROBIT_LINK` {#PROBIT_LINK}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:PROBIT_LINK -->
```python
PROBIT_LINK = Link(name='probit', link=(lambda mu: _norm.ppf(jnp.clip(mu, _EPS, 1.0 - _EPS))), linkinv=_probit_linkinv, mu_eta=_probit_mu_eta)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:PROBIT_LINK -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `CLOGLOG_LINK` {#CLOGLOG_LINK}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:CLOGLOG_LINK -->
```python
CLOGLOG_LINK = Link(name='cloglog', link=_cloglog_link, linkinv=_cloglog_linkinv, mu_eta=_cloglog_mu_eta, eta_bound=_ETA_MAX)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:CLOGLOG_LINK -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `SQRT_LINK` {#SQRT_LINK}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:SQRT_LINK -->
```python
SQRT_LINK = Link(name='sqrt', link=(lambda mu: jnp.sqrt(jnp.clip(mu, 0.0, None))), linkinv=(lambda eta: eta * eta), mu_eta=(lambda eta: 2.0 * eta))
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:SQRT_LINK -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `INVERSE_LINK` {#INVERSE_LINK}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:INVERSE_LINK -->
```python
INVERSE_LINK = Link(name='inverse', link=(lambda mu: 1.0 / jnp.clip(mu, _EPS, None)), linkinv=(lambda eta: 1.0 / jnp.clip(eta, _EPS, None)), mu_eta=_inverse_mu_eta, eta_bound=(float('inf')))
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:INVERSE_LINK -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `resolve_family` {#resolve_family}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:resolve_family -->
```python
resolve_family(family: Union[str, Family]) -> Family
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:resolve_family -->
Resolve a `str` name (a built-in) or a `Family` instance to a family.

`glm_fit` / `gam_fit` accept either; a string is looked up in the
built-in registry (`'gaussian'` / `'binomial'` / `'poisson'` /
`'gamma'` / `'negbinomial'`).  `'negbinomial'` resolves to the default
`alpha = 1`; for another dispersion pass `negbinomial(alpha)` directly.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:resolve_family -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `family` | `Union[str, Family]` | — | — | — |

**Returns** `Family`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `resolve_link` {#resolve_link}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:resolve_link -->
```python
resolve_link(link: Union[str, Link]) -> Link
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:resolve_link -->
Resolve a `str` name or a :class:`Link` instance to a :class:`Link`.

Built-ins: `'identity'` / `'log'` / `'logit'` / `'probit'` /
`'cloglog'` / `'sqrt'` / `'inverse'`; or pass a :class:`Link` directly.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:resolve_link -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `link` | `Union[str, Link]` | — | — | — |

**Returns** `Link`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `GAUSSIAN` {#GAUSSIAN}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:GAUSSIAN -->
```python
GAUSSIAN = Family(name='gaussian', has_fixed_dispersion=False, link=_identity, linkinv=_identity, mu_eta=_ones_like, variance=_ones_like, unit_deviance=(lambda y, mu: (y - mu) ** 2), init_mu=_identity, loglik=_gaussian_loglik)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:GAUSSIAN -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `BINOMIAL` {#BINOMIAL}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:BINOMIAL -->
```python
BINOMIAL = Family(name='binomial', has_fixed_dispersion=True, link=_logit, linkinv=_expit, mu_eta=_binomial_mu_eta, variance=(lambda mu: mu * (1.0 - mu)), unit_deviance=_binomial_deviance, init_mu=(lambda y: (y + 0.5) / 2.0), loglik=_binomial_loglik)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:BINOMIAL -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `POISSON` {#POISSON}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:POISSON -->
```python
POISSON = Family(name='poisson', has_fixed_dispersion=True, link=(lambda mu: jnp.log(jnp.clip(mu, _EPS, None))), linkinv=_safe_exp, mu_eta=_safe_exp, variance=_identity, unit_deviance=_poisson_deviance, init_mu=(lambda y: y + 0.1), loglik=_poisson_loglik, eta_bound=_ETA_MAX)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:POISSON -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `GAMMA` {#GAMMA}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:GAMMA -->
```python
GAMMA = Family(name='gamma', has_fixed_dispersion=False, link=_log_link, linkinv=_safe_exp, mu_eta=_safe_exp, variance=(lambda mu: mu * mu), unit_deviance=_gamma_deviance, init_mu=(lambda y: jnp.clip(y, _EPS, None)), loglik=_gamma_loglik, eta_bound=_ETA_MAX)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:GAMMA -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `NEGBINOMIAL` {#NEGBINOMIAL}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:NEGBINOMIAL -->
```python
NEGBINOMIAL = negbinomial(1.0)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:NEGBINOMIAL -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `TWEEDIE` {#TWEEDIE}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:TWEEDIE -->
```python
TWEEDIE = tweedie(1.5)
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:TWEEDIE -->
*TODO: describe this type alias / constant.*
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `negbinomial` {#negbinomial}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:negbinomial -->
```python
negbinomial(alpha: float = 1.0) -> Family
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:negbinomial -->
A negative-binomial (NB2) family with **known** dispersion `alpha`.

NB2 parameterisation: `V(mu) = mu + alpha * mu^2` (overdispersed counts),
log link.  `alpha` is fixed at construction -- with it known, NB2 is a
proper one-parameter family (GLM dispersion 1), so the shared IRLS core fits
it directly.  Jointly *estimating* `alpha` (the profile-MLE outer loop) is
a documented follow-up.  `alpha -> 0` recovers Poisson.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:negbinomial -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `alpha` | `float` | — | `1.0` | — |

**Returns** `Family`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `tweedie` {#tweedie}

*Re-exported here; defined in `nitrix.stats._family`.*

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:tweedie -->
```python
tweedie(p: float = 1.5) -> Family
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:tweedie -->
A Tweedie family with **fixed** power `p` (compound Poisson-Gamma).

`1 < p < 2` -- the semicontinuous regime (an exact zero with probability
mass plus a continuous positive part: rainfall, insurance claims, some
imaging measures).  Variance `V(mu) = mu^p`, log link, dispersion estimated
(`has_fixed_dispersion=False`, like Gamma).  With `p` fixed the *mean*
coefficients fit the shared IRLS core directly (working weight `mu^{2-p}`);
jointly profiling `p` is a follow-up (`p -> 1` recovers Poisson, `p -> 2`
Gamma).

The unit deviance is the closed form

    d(y, mu) = 2[ y^{2-p}/((1-p)(2-p)) - y mu^{1-p}/(1-p) + mu^{2-p}/(2-p) ];

the log-likelihood uses the **saddlepoint approximation** (with the exact
compound-Poisson zero mass at `y = 0`) -- enough for `aic` / `bic` model
comparison; the exact Dunn-Smyth series is the follow-up.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:tweedie -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `p` | `float` | — | `1.5` | — |

**Returns** `Family`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `GLMResult` {#GLMResult}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:GLMResult -->
```python
class GLMResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:GLMResult -->
Per-element GLM fit output.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

### Methods

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:GLMResult.__init__ -->
```python
__init__(coef: Float[Array, 'V p'], cov_unscaled: Float[Array, '... p p'], dispersion: Float[Array, 'V'], deviance: Float[Array, 'V'], null_deviance: Float[Array, 'V'], log_lik: Float[Array, 'V'], family: Family, n_obs: int, rank: int) -> None
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `glm_fit` {#glm_fit}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:glm_fit -->
```python
glm_fit(Y: Float[Array, 'V N'], X: Float[Array, 'N p'], *, family: Union[str, Family] = GAUSSIAN, weights: Optional[Float[Array, 'N']] = None, rank: Optional[int] = None, n_iter: int = 25, ridge: float = 0.0, block: Optional[int] = None) -> GLMResult
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:glm_fit -->
Fit a mass-univariate GLM: shared design `X`, per-element responses.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:glm_fit -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Y` | `Float` | `V` × `N` | — | `(V, N)` responses -- `V` elements, `N` observations. |
| `X` | `Float` | `N` × `p` | — | `(N, p)` design, shared across elements (include your own intercept column -- no intercept is added). |
| `family` | `Union[str, Family]` | — | `GAUSSIAN` | Exponential family + link: a built-in name (`'gaussian'` / `'binomial'` / `'poisson'`) or a `Family` instance. Default `GAUSSIAN` identity = OLS. |
| `weights` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N']]` | — | `None` | Optional `(N,)` prior observation weights (WLS / known precision). `None` is unweighted. |
| `rank` | `Optional[int]` | — | `None` | Effective rank of `X` for the residual degrees of freedom (dispersion / SE / AIC). `None` (default) detects it host-side (`matrix_rank`) when `X` is concrete, falling back to `p` under `jax.jit`; pass it explicitly for a known rank-deficient design traced under `jit`. |
| `n_iter` | `int` | — | `25` | IRLS iterations for non-Gaussian families (ignored on the OLS path). |
| `ridge` | `float` | — | `0.0` | Optional L2 stabiliser added to the normal-equation matrix. |
| `block` | `Optional[int]` | — | `None` | Optional element-block size bounding peak memory on the per-element IRLS path (non-Gaussian families). `None` (default) is a single `vmap`; the OLS fast path is already vectorised and ignores it. |

**Returns** `GLMResult`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `predict` {#predict}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:predict -->
```python
predict(result: GLMResult, X: Float[Array, 'N p'], *, type: PredictType = 'response') -> Float[Array, 'V N']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:predict -->
Per-element prediction on a (new) design `X`.

`type='link'` returns the linear predictor `eta = X beta`;
`type='response'` (default) applies the inverse link.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:predict -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GLMResult` | — | — | — |
| `X` | `Float` | `N` × `p` | — | — |
| `type` | `PredictType` | — | `'response'` | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `V` × `N`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `t_contrast` {#t_contrast}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:t_contrast -->
```python
t_contrast(result: GLMResult, contrast: Float[Array, 'p'], *, cov: Optional[Float[Array, '... p p']] = None) -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'V'], Float[Array, 'V'], Float[Array, 'V'], Float[Array, 'V']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:t_contrast -->
Per-element t-test of a linear contrast `c^T beta`.

Returns `(effect, se, t, p_value)`: the contrast estimate, its standard
error, the t statistic, and the two-sided p-value at `dof_resid` degrees
of freedom (ModelArray `lm` columns `estimate` / `std.error` /
`statistic` / `p.value`).

`cov` overrides the coefficient covariance: pass a `(V, p, p)` (or shared
`(p, p)`) matrix -- e.g. the robust `sandwich_cov` output -- to use
`c^T cov c` directly (already on the variance scale).  The default
(`None`) is the model-based `dispersion * (X^T W X)^{-1}` (unchanged).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:t_contrast -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GLMResult` | — | — | — |
| `contrast` | `Float` | `p` | — | — |
| `cov` | `Optional[Float[Array, '... p p']]` | — | `None` | — |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'V'], Float[Array, 'V'], Float[Array, 'V'], Float[Array, 'V']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `f_contrast` {#f_contrast}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:f_contrast -->
```python
f_contrast(result: GLMResult, contrast: Float[Array, 'm p'], *, cov: Optional[Float[Array, '... p p']] = None) -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'V'], Float[Array, 'V'], float, float]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:f_contrast -->
Per-element F-test of a multi-row contrast `C beta = 0`.

Returns `(F, p_value, df1, df2)` with `df1 = m` (contrast rank) and
`df2 = dof_resid`.

`cov` overrides the coefficient covariance (e.g. the robust
`sandwich_cov` output): the Wald quadratic form uses `C cov C^T` directly
and the statistic is `quad / m` (no dispersion rescale).  The default
(`None`) is the model-based `dispersion * (X^T W X)^{-1}` (unchanged).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:f_contrast -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GLMResult` | — | — | — |
| `contrast` | `Float` | `m` × `p` | — | — |
| `cov` | `Optional[Float[Array, '... p p']]` | — | `None` | — |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'V'], Float[Array, 'V'], float, float]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `sandwich_cov` {#sandwich_cov}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:sandwich_cov -->
```python
sandwich_cov(result: GLMResult, Y: Float[Array, 'V N'], X: Float[Array, 'N p'], *, kind: HCKind = 'HC3', groups: Optional[Int[Array, 'N']] = None, n_groups: Optional[int] = None, weights: Optional[Float[Array, 'N']] = None) -> Float[Array, 'V p p']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:sandwich_cov -->
Robust (sandwich) coefficient covariance for a fitted GLM.

Returns the per-element `(V, p, p)` covariance
`A^{-1} B A^{-1}` with bread `A^{-1} = (X^T W X)^{-1}` (the fit's
`cov_unscaled`) and a robust meat `B` built from the per-observation
score contributions `u_i = x_i g_i`, `g_i = (y_i - mu_i) (dmu_i/deta_i) /
V(mu_i)` (for OLS, `g_i` is the raw residual `y_i - mu_i` -- the
classic Huber-White form).  cuSOLVER-free, `vmap` over elements; the
sandwich weights are data-dependent constants (not differentiated through).

Pass the result as `cov=` to `t_contrast` / `f_contrast`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:sandwich_cov -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GLMResult` | — | — | The fitted `GLMResult` and the data it was fit on (`X` shared, `Y` the `(V, N)` responses). |
| `Y` | `Float` | `V` × `N` | — | The fitted `GLMResult` and the data it was fit on (`X` shared, `Y` the `(V, N)` responses). |
| `X` | `Float` | `N` × `p` | — | The fitted `GLMResult` and the data it was fit on (`X` shared, `Y` the `(V, N)` responses). |
| `kind` | `HCKind` | — | `'HC3'` | Heteroscedasticity-consistent variant when `groups` is `None`: `'HC0'` (raw), `'HC1'` (`n/(n-p)` correction), `'HC2'` (`/(1 - h_i)`), `'HC3'` (`/(1 - h_i)^2`); `h_i` the GLM hat diagonal. Ignored when `groups` is given. |
| `groups` | `Optional[Int[Array, 'N']]` | — | `None` | `(N,)` cluster labels for a **cluster-robust** covariance (one-way): `B = sum_g s_g s_g^T`, `s_g = sum_{i in g} x_i g_i`, with the `G/(G-1) * (N-1)/(N-p)` small-sample factor. This is **one-way** clustering -- FSL `randomise`'s `-e` exchangeability-block analogue (audit N6). It is **not** a variance-group model (FSL `-g` / PALM `-vg`): a separate residual *variance* per group (heteroscedastic two-sample / FLAME-style grouped variance) is a different estimator and is not provided here -- the cluster-robust meat assumes a common model, only correlating scores *within* a cluster. The cluster path is **eager-only** by default: the distinct-cluster count is found with `jnp.unique` (a data-dependent shape). To trace the whole estimator under `jax.jit`, pre-densify `groups` to contiguous `0..n_groups-1` labels and pass `n_groups`. |
| `n_groups` | `Optional[int]` | — | `None` | Optional distinct-cluster count. `None` (default) densifies `groups` host-side; supplying it (with contiguous labels) keeps `sandwich_cov` jit-traceable. Ignored when `groups` is `None`. |
| `weights` | `Optional[Float[Array, 'N']]` | — | `None` | Optional `(N,)` prior weights matching the fit's `weights=`. |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `V` × `p` × `p`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `r_squared` {#r_squared}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:r_squared -->
```python
r_squared(result: GLMResult) -> Float[Array, 'V']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:r_squared -->
Per-element `R^2` (Gaussian) / deviance explained (general).

For the Gaussian family `deviance` is the residual sum of squares and
`null_deviance` the total sum of squares, so this is the ordinary
`R^2`; for other families it is the fraction of deviance explained.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:r_squared -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GLMResult` | — | — | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `V`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `adj_r_squared` {#adj_r_squared}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:adj_r_squared -->
```python
adj_r_squared(result: GLMResult) -> Float[Array, 'V']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:adj_r_squared -->
Per-element adjusted `R^2` (Gaussian).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:adj_r_squared -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GLMResult` | — | — | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `V`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `deviance_explained` {#deviance_explained}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:deviance_explained -->
```python
deviance_explained(result: GLMResult) -> Float[Array, 'V']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:deviance_explained -->
Per-element fraction of null deviance explained: `1 - dev / null_dev`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:deviance_explained -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GLMResult` | — | — | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `V`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `log_likelihood` {#log_likelihood}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:log_likelihood -->
```python
log_likelihood(result: GLMResult) -> Float[Array, 'V']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:log_likelihood -->
Per-element maximised log-likelihood.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:log_likelihood -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GLMResult` | — | — | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `V`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `aic` {#aic}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:aic -->
```python
aic(result: GLMResult) -> Float[Array, 'V']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:aic -->
Per-element Akaike information criterion `-2 ll + 2 k`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:aic -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GLMResult` | — | — | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `V`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `bic` {#bic}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:bic -->
```python
bic(result: GLMResult) -> Float[Array, 'V']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:bic -->
Per-element Bayesian information criterion `-2 ll + k log N`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:bic -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `result` | `GLMResult` | — | — | — |

**Returns** `Float` — shape `V`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## `compare_models` {#compare_models}

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:compare_models -->
```python
compare_models(full: GLMResult, reduced: GLMResult, *, test: CompareTest = 'auto') -> Tuple[Float[Array, 'V'], Float[Array, 'V']]
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:compare_models -->
Per-element nested-model comparison (ModelArray `full vs. reduced`).

`reduced` must be nested in `full` (fewer parameters, same data).
Returns `(statistic, p_value)`:

- `test='F'` (or `'auto'` for a Gaussian family) -- the extra-sum-of-
  squares F-test, `F = ((D_r - D_f) / (k_f - k_r)) / (D_f / dof_f)` on
  `(k_f - k_r, dof_f)` degrees of freedom.
- `test='LRT'` (or `'auto'` otherwise) -- the likelihood-ratio test,
  `2 (ll_f - ll_r) ~ chi^2(k_f - k_r)`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:compare_models -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `full` | `GLMResult` | — | — | — |
| `reduced` | `GLMResult` | — | — | — |
| `test` | `CompareTest` | — | `'auto'` | — |

**Returns** `Tuple[Float[Array, 'V'], Float[Array, 'V']]`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
